key_points: The real number system consists of rational and irrational numbers
Rational numbers have decimal expansions that either terminate or repeat infinitely
Irrational numbers have non-terminating and non-repeating decimal expansions
Square roots of non-perfect squares are irrational numbers
Simplification of radicals uses prime factorization
Real numbers can be represented as points on the number line
formulas: Rational decimal rule → terminating or repeating
exam_tips: Use prime factorization to simplify square roots
Identify whether decimal expansions terminate or repeat
Practice writing radicals in simplest form
Remember that irrational numbers cannot be written as fractions
